item.dc:creator: tentaclefriendly

item.title: 41lbs over 2 years
item.dc:creator: BnDD

item.title: The Return of the Meme (vol. 453)
item.description:
Good Morning Imgur! Enjoy today's selection.
I refer to myself as a Wine Goblin on occasion.
Yeah, I'm going to try and not do this from now on.
I do like to use Garamond.
I would totally put this in my home.
Word on the street is that there's a new redescription or examination of new specimens of Ouranosaurus on the way. I'm stoked.
Prehistoric sharks got into some strange body plans.
Okay. This is going to require some explanation. So: Troodon was originally known from teeth, and was thought to be a predator. Then someone found a pachycephalosaur with what looked like the same teeth and so everyone was all "ah! Okay! We were wrong!" So for a while everyone was like Troodon was a carnivorous ornithopod! Then someone else found a skull of a small meat-eating dinosaur that was first named Stenonychosaurus. And then it was like.. oh, this has the actual same teeth so it was a therapod the whole time. I'm sorry your eyes glazed over there.
Yeah, they don't really know what to do with Her.
I am worthy. I can treat myself to a nice thing now and again. So are you, friend.
The fact that I joked seriously that my last ex and I had a relationship like Geralt and Yen should have been a *HUGE* red flag. But, like Yennifer, she was hot, had a British accent, and a fantastic figure.
Fusion? Boiling water even better.
I am a great admirer of Siesta Culture.
Or use headphones.
I'm looking at you, Mantis Shrimp guy. And I just want to say: Thank you for correcting me. I don't want to share false information.
Sometimes on tours when we only see one or two species of birds, and my guests are disappointed, I remind them that Birds are Dinosaurs, and we saw some. Instant mood lifter.
The quality has improved.
At least three friends IRL sent this to me, saying "this is you at parties"
Us. Thankfully.
Mood.
My mind immediately thinks this would be a great label for a Washington cabernet sauvignon.
I didn't encounter dinosaurs the last time I did mushrooms.
Pippin tax!
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett

item.title: ancient artifacts
item.description:
1..... https://www.isu.edu/media/libraries/rhi/research-papers/Mayak-Datat-Hairy-Man-Pictographs-1.pdf
2..... https://www.history.com/topics/landmarks/serpent-mound
3..... https://www.christies.com/en/lot/lot-4505441
4..... https://joyofmuseums.com/museums/middle-east-museums/turkey-museums/istanbul-museums/istanbul-archaeology-museums/alexander-sarcophagus/
5..... https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Caral
6..... https://www.artnews.com/art-news/news/roman-glass-bowl-nijmegen-1234616630/
7..... - https://www.smithsonianmag.com/smart-news/these-200000-year-old-hand-and-footprints-could-be-the-worlds-oldest-cave-art-180978702/   - https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2095927321006174
8..... https://www.reddit.com/r/ArtefactPorn/comments/sr8v4t/comment/hwqe65s/?utm_source=share&utm_medium=web2x&c
9..... https://www.bl.uk/magna-carta/articles/magna-carta-english-translation
10..... https://whc.unesco.org/en/list/1654
11..... https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Joan_of_Leeds
12..... https://earthobservatory.nasa.gov/images/7606/chankillo-observatory-peru
13..... https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Berlin_Gold_Hat
14..... https://haber.sol.org.tr/haber/karahantepede-11-bin-yillik-heykel-bulundu-326366
15..... https://themedievalhunt.com/2015/03/19/the-lappvattnet-hat/
16..... https://www.lib.cam.ac.uk/genizah-fragments/posts/how-did-jewish-children-learn-write-thousand-years-ago
item.dc:creator: theshopliftingseagull

item.title: So refreshing
item.description:
like I've said before, I live in a small rural conservative town. I doubt I would catch much flack going out crossdressed but I just dont think im ready. I've been cooped up in this house too long. So I dressed up, drove over an hour to KC and went to a gay bar and literally everyone there was super cool. We just talked about our lives, stuff we done, jobs we had and there was literally no stress. Heck, even had someone buy me and a couple other girls shots, lol. I had nerves like crazy going in but the bartender met me with a smile and made me feel right at home and all that stress just melted away. I just wanted to share for anyone else in my situation. hope you take the chance, find a place with good reviews and go do your thing in a safe space. it will help so much.
cat tax.
item.dc:creator: XstrangeduckX

item.title: Webcomics Part VI
item.description:
Sorry for the lack of sauce here. Its Pitch black by Andre Navarro
Again, no credit in the image. Not 100% sure but possibly first posted to halolz.com
I love webcomics and picked these 50 at random from my phone folder. Sources should be in the images. All credit to the amazing artists and writers who created these!

item.title: Emotions overflowing in Finnish
item.description: Torille. edit. Most viral?! Wow, I can see the emotions are contagious! To be fair, Jukka Jalonen was a bit choked up in the interviev, he had to pause for a bit when thanking his family, to not let any of that sweet emotion leak out and show.
item.dc:creator: NuclearGradeIrony

item.title: Help
item.description: My dad died last night. Don't know what happened. We think it was an aneurysm. send anything. please. just make the pain stop for even a second Edit: Thank you all, wonderful Imgur community. I just finished the first half of the drive back to my parent's and saw all of your support. Thank you all for making a ray of hope in my otherwise horrible day
item.dc:creator: LogicKitty

item.title: Dear Republicans, be more vocal with those you ally￼ with￼
item.dc:creator: MissySassy

item.title: Goblin Dad Plays with Goblin Daughter
item.description:
By Brian Campise. Side comic featuring characters from our webcomic Arru and the Magical Mask. We post the comic on Webtoon on a weekly basis: https://www.webtoons.com/en/challenge/arru-and-the-magical-mask/list?title_no=632264&page=1 If you'd like to support us, we also have a Patreon here: https://www.patreon.com/campside
Also, we just posted our entire comic on tapas in three big convenient chunks (75 pages total) just to YOLO it and see what happens. We also plan on updating the comic there on a monthly basis instead of a weekly one. So if you prefer your comic updates chunky, this is the place to go. https://tapas.io/episode/2454727
item.dc:creator: MarcusSimius

item.title: Office Renovation
item.description:
The Money Shot! All Done!
Camera 1.
Camera 2.
The Before. My garage is right next to this room. This room actually opened to the garage on the left wall at one point. A previous owner walled it up and opened it to the inside to create an office. Then they installed this behemoth of a desk. You can't really tell but it took up half the office. And that is an cheap hollow core door for the desktop.
Ripped the shelves out without much damage.
This was my first time removing the acoustic texture. This room doesn't have any natural light and isn't very visible. I figured it's the best room to get my practice. I was very surprised how easy it came off. Didn't gouge the ceiling at all.
It made an absolute mess. Really should have laid something down in hindsight obviously. Fortunately it vacuumed up fine without any residual mess.
There was an old fluorescent garage light in here. I wanted to add something that would give a softer light. Also since there isn't any ventilation I want to put in a fan to circulate the air. The original box was not fan rated so I opened up the ceiling to put in a fan rated box.
New box, wires run, and patched.
Mudded every nick and spot I saw. This was my first mess up. Due to limited light I didn't see all the blemishes until I painted.
Thanks to flat ceiling paint you can't really see any of the spots unless you look really hard. The trip here is lots of light so you don't miss any blemishes.
Patched the walls where the desk was. Wasn't much to patch really. I had a very elegant work lamp for this project as you can see.
New fan and light installed. This thing was quite heavy so that new fan box was necessary especially since I will be sitting right under it.
Trim coats going down. Antique/Retro white.
Walls Painted. The color is Sherwin Williams Ethereal Mood.
Early morning trip to Lowes for wood for the floating shelves.
Supports in for the floating desk and shelves. About half the screws are in studs and half are not but used heavy duty drywall anchors where not.
Added a cross brace in the corners for extra support.
Began adding the additional shelf supports.
Shelves are wrapped! I used. Tops are 1x10. Fronts are 1x4. Bottom is 1/3 inch plywood. The shelves are mitred in the corners. Second mess up. I didn't have enough attachment points for the plywood and it bends. So in a few spots it sags just slightly. It's barely noticeable but in hindsight I should have attached a few spots on the fascia board so I could nail into. I didn't have a nail gun at the tile so everything it's all just attached with finishing nails by hammer.
Cut the plywood 3/4 in. plywood for the desktop and dry fit it before I began attaching and staining. It's mitred in the corner.
Stain and poly going down. Used Minwax provincial. I picked it because it seemed to match the floor most. Turned out to be a bit darker but not too noticeable.
All stained. Third mess up. I had this file cabinet prior to moving in this house. I wanted some storage so decided it would be a good solution. I measured twice so I could slide it out if necessary but it's not coming out. The desktop fascia is about 1/2 lower than I intended. Not a big deal, I've had zero need to move it out and there is enough wiggle room to run wires and the top drawer opens fine.
Everything poly'd. In hingsight I would have done something different for the desktop. Just would have liked a contrast but this was the most cost effective option by far. Also needed to be light weight which it is. I've considered coming back with some type of epoxy treatment but have moved on to other projects. After everything was dry I used a 2 in hole saw and drilled holes in the corner and ends of the desktop and shelves. Bought some cheap desk grommets from Amazon to fill the holes.
I wanted to add some small accents to the shelves so I purchased these black automotive bolts and spaced them out on the shelves.
A few years before this project I scored an office grade whiteboard that was being tossed at the place I worked. I removed the metal frame and build a wood frame to match.
Just screwed the frame to the whiteboard from the backside.
Whiteboard mounted with a little help. I honestly thought I'd use this thing all the time. The kids draw on it more than I do by far. The outlet is the only outlet in the office. In hindsight I wish I had installed another below the desk.
My solution for wiring was to buy to 10 outlet surge protectors with 15 foot cords. I build a small shelf from scraps, painted them black, mounted it high enough to be out of sight but low enough to plug in large adapters. I then mounted the surge protectors to them. There is enough room on the backside to lay wires and then used some cord clips to pin up hanging wires.
item.dc:creator: braalewi

item.title: as a Christian, if I was given a Bible he signed, I would tear the cover off
item.dc:creator: reformedxile

item.title: I'm in a lot of pain
item.description: Gf and I have had a tough week. They hurt my feelings and I established a clear boundary in our relationship. They crossed it and it cost the relationship. Its early and none of my support friends are awake, so just thought I'd shout into the void that I'm in a lot of pain rn. Edit: I appreciate yalls support. Honestly. I'm feeling many emotions, the largest of which is anger that I let myself get walked on for so long. The relationship has been toxic for a while, but now it went too far. I made it crystal clear I had a reasonable boundary, and they crossed it. I hope you guys have a lovely day, or at least better than mine
item.dc:creator: Notfunnycommenter
